    "R1.2": "The section asserts 'uniformly improved execution accuracy across the full suite' while its own numbers contradict this: WikiSQL shows RouteSQL at 89.1% vs the baseline's 89.6%, and SParC shows 64.2% vs 65.1% — both losses. KaggleDBQA reports no baseline at all. Logical coherence between the summary claim and the tabulated evidence is central to any substantive review of this section.",
    "R1.3": "Evidence-claim alignment is the most critically exposed dimension: the headline accuracy claim is directly falsified by two of five benchmark rows in the same paragraph. A 'strictly dominant' strategy cannot be supported when the artifact's own evidence shows the baseline winning on two datasets and missing on a third. This dim is nearly load-bearing regardless of review type.",
    "R1.6": "'Review this section' plausibly targets prose quality; the section contains overconfident superlatives ('uniformly improved', 'strictly dominant', 'strongest deployment-ready system') whose tone warrants copyediting independent of whether their underlying logic is also flagged."

  "clarification_question": "Should this review focus primarily on content accuracy — verifying that the reported numbers logically support the stated claims — or on writing quality and prose clarity, treating the numbers as given?"

rationale.R1.2: The section asserts 'uniformly improved execution accuracy across the full suite' while its own numbers contradict this: WikiSQL shows RouteSQL at 89.1% vs the baseline's 89.6%, and SParC shows 64.2% vs 65.1% — both losses. KaggleDBQA reports no baseline at all. Logical coherence between the summary claim and the tabulated evidence is central to any substantive review of this section.
rationale.R1.3: Evidence-claim alignment is the most critically exposed dimension: the headline accuracy claim is directly falsified by two of five benchmark rows in the same paragraph. A 'strictly dominant' strategy cannot be supported when the artifact's own evidence shows the baseline winning on two datasets and missing on a third. This dim is nearly load-bearing regardless of review type.
rationale.R1.6: 'Review this section' plausibly targets prose quality; the section contains overconfident superlatives ('uniformly improved', 'strictly dominant', 'strongest deployment-ready system') whose tone warrants copyediting independent of whether their underlying logic is also flagged.

parsed.clarification_question: Should this review focus primarily on content accuracy — verifying that the reported numbers logically support the stated claims — or on writing quality and prose clarity, treating the numbers as given?
